title: Framing Messages to Deal With the COVID-19 Crisis: The Role of Loss/Gain Frames and Content
population: Convenience sample 18-60yr Colombian Spanish speakers from university mailing lists + Facebook
operationalization_verbatim: 2 Frame (gain/loss) × 2 Content (health/economy) within-subjects design with 8 message pairs presented sequentially. Gain-framed messages emphasized positive outcomes of behaviors (hand washing physical distancing staying home); loss-framed messages emphasized negative consequences of not adopting behaviors. Health content versus economic content.
rob_notes: Adequate randomization to condition; blinding not reported; attrition not reported; low selective-reporting risk due to OSF pre-registration with public materials. Singular-fit models analyzed as GLMs omitting random effects. Manipulation checks showed >91% recognition.

title: An Experimental Study on Anchoring Effect of Consumers' Price Judgment Based on Consumers' Experiencing Scenes
population: Ordinary consumers age 18+ from authorized Huawei experience store in Chengdu
operationalization_verbatim: High anchor: 'whether the price of VR glasses is higher than 6000 yuan or lower' (2x actual price). Low anchor: 'whether the price of VR glasses is higher than 1500 yuan or lower' (0.5x actual). Internal anchor: no external reference.
rob_notes: Randomization not clearly described; no blinding mentioned; attrition noted (194 of 270 participated; ~28% attrition); small sample acknowledged

title: The effect of partitioned framing vs. all-inclusive framing of donation amount on prosocial behavior
population: Amazon Mechanical Turk workers; 40.5% female; age distributed 20-60
operationalization_verbatim: Partitioned: 'California wildfire $4 / Hurricane $5 / Earthquake $3 / Total $12'. All-inclusive: 'Donation amount $12 / Donation details: CA wildfire, Hurricane, Earthquake'
rob_notes: Random assignment stated but method not detailed; blinding not reported; attrition not reported; selective reporting not apparent

title: The Effect of Online Social Proof Regarding Organic Food: Comments and Likes on Facebook
population: Dutch psychology students 25M/63F mean age 21 participating for course credit
operationalization_verbatim: Positive condition: 'many likes (>300) were given to positive statements regarding organic foods and few likes (<20) to negative ones'. Reversed in negative condition.
funding: Netherlands Food and Consumer Product Safety Authority
rob_notes: Random assignment stated; method not detailed; substantial attrition (145 enrolled -> 88 analyzed = 39% attrition via manipulation-check exclusion; high RoB on attrition); blinding not reported

title: Influence of the Framing Effect Anchoring Effect and Knowledge on Consumers Attitude and Purchase Intention of Organic Food
population: Chinese consumers from 5 districts of Wuxi mean age 26-55 (57.6%); 74.5% college-educated; 37.2% male
operationalization_verbatim: Positive framing: 'Organic food uses natural and ecological production methods...benefiting everyone. When you decide to purchase organic lettuce you are making a healthy decision'. Negative framing: 'When you decide against purchasing organic lettuce you are making an unhealthy decision'. Low anchor 3 RMB; High anchor 15 RMB (15th and 85th quantiles from pre-survey).
effect_size_value: Negative-vs-positive frame attitude: M=4.461 vs M=3.580 F=61.430; Low-vs-high anchor: M=4.591 vs M=3.446 F=56.728
funding: National Natural Science Foundation of China (71603104
extraction_date: Random assignment stated; method not specified; 8% attrition (32 of 400 excluded); selective reporting status unknown; high power N=368

title: When How and Why is Loss-Framing More Effective than Gain- and Non-Gain-Framing in the Promotion of Detection Behaviors
operationalization_verbatim: Gain: 'By taking the diagnostic blood test you can find out your current cholesterol level'. Non-gain: 'By not taking the diagnostic blood test you will not find out your current cholesterol level'. Loss: 'By not taking the diagnostic blood test you will ignore your current cholesterol level'.
effect_size_value: Loss-vs-gain/non-gain contrast: beta=0.25 t(222)=4.71; Interaction with Personal Relevance: beta=0.26 t(222)=4.79; Indirect effect through response-efficacy: ab=0.16 95% CI [0.09, 0.25]
rob_notes: Random allocation stated; method not specified; attrition not reported; no pre-registration; relatively small per-cell N

title: A Matching Study on the Influence of Advertised Information Expression and Product Type on Consumer Purchase Intention
population: Chinese undergrad/grad students 53.8% male; corrected vision >1.5
operationalization_verbatim: Mobile phone = search product; facial mask = experience product. Direct expression: 'simple natural easy to understand'. Metaphorical: 'implicit interesting stimulates consumer imagination'.
effect_size_value: Interaction F(1,396)=51.72; experience-metaphor M=4.93 vs experience-direct M=3.99 F=23.01; search-direct M=4.92 vs search-metaphor M=3.76 F=28.74
funding: Philosophy and Social Science Research Project of Tianjin (TJGL21-002)
rob_notes: Randomization not detailed; blinding not reported; no attrition info; selective reporting not assessed

title: Climate Change From a Distance: An Analysis of Construal Level and Psychological Distance From Climate Change
population: Australian adults via Qualtrics mean age 43.48 (range 18-79); 105 female; median income $35-50k
operationalization_verbatim: No intervention - participants completed PD1 PD2 BIF-E BIF-G RCW + environmental behavior measures
rob_notes: STAGE-2 EXCLUDE: correlational design fails §2.5 (random assignment or quasi-experimental with identification strategy required); self-report; no intervention; serves only as descriptive coverage

title: Gain-Framed Messaging for Promoting Adult Sport: Examining the Effects of Efficacy-Enhancing Information
population: Adult Canadians 30-69 yr mean 47.0 (SD=8.6); 62.5% female; did not perceive sport as risky (≤5 on 7-pt scale)
operationalization_verbatim: 4-min narrated PowerPoint with 9 consecutive GFM each highlighting a benefit of adult sport (optimal health delayed aging social affiliation fun stress-relief physical-capability thrills competitive-goals travel)
effect_size_value: OE-Travel largest: F(1,219)=12.3 etap²=0.05; no significant behavioral effects
funding: Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada
rob_notes: High overall attrition (51.1%); selective dropout favored less-active; high experimental-group dropout (40.4% vs control 19.3%); seasonal collection confound (Nov-Jan); high attrition RoB
